166.reason: [Error: After recording the error for a single '|', the method does not return a token, causing an infinite loop. Should return a token or skip the character.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: After recording the error for a single '|', the method does not return a token, causing an infinite loop. Should return a token or skip the character.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Same issue as above - no token returned or character consumed after error]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Same issue here: for standalone '|', the error is added but no token is returned, causing the same problem.

92.reason: The condition fails when index > line.length() (which happens after getCharNow reads past the end of the line), causing an infinite loop because nextLine() is never called to reset index and fetch new content.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition fails when index > line.length() (which happens after getCharNow reads past the end of the line), causing an infinite loop because nextLine() is never called to reset index and fetch new content.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition `index == line.length()` fails to handle the case where `index` exceeds `line.length()` (i.e., `index == line.length() + 1`). This can happen because `getCharNow()` increments `index` even when it detects the end of the line (setting `charNow` to `(char)-1`). If the line ends with whitespace, the whitespace-skipping loop calls `getCharNow()`, which sets `index` to `line.length() + 1`. Consequently, this `if` block is skipped, `nextLine()` is not called, and the inner `while` loop condition `index < line.length()` is also false, causing an infinite loop in the outer `while(true)` structure.
